name: Domain-Driven Design

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: domain-driven-design Domain-Driven Design  A software design approach that uses

summary: A software design approach that uses business learning, language, and boundaries to decide what to model deeply and what to keep simple.

name: Strategic Design

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: strategic-design Strategic Design  The DDD layer that answers what business area

summary: The DDD layer that answers what business area matters, why it matters, and where model and team boundaries should sit.

name: Tactical Design

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: tactical-design Tactical Design  The DDD layer that chooses implementation patte

summary: The DDD layer that chooses implementation patterns for business logic once the strategic boundary is understood.

name: Business Domain

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: business-domain Business Domain  The business activity area whose goals, constra

summary: The business activity area whose goals, constraints, and economic logic should shape software boundaries and model depth.

name: Core Subdomain

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: core-subdomain Core Subdomain  A differentiating business capability that deserv

summary: A differentiating business capability that deserves the most modeling attention because it materially affects competitive advantage.

name: Supporting Subdomain

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: supporting-subdomain Supporting Subdomain  A business capability that supports t

summary: A business capability that supports the company but is usually less differentiating than the core and should receive proportionate engineering investment.

name: Generic Subdomain

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: generic-subdomain Generic Subdomain  A common capability many organizations solv

summary: A common capability many organizations solve similarly; it is often a buy, outsource, or boring-build candidate.

name: Domain Expert

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: domain-expert Domain Expert  A business-side knowledge holder whose language, ex

summary: A business-side knowledge holder whose language, examples, and edge cases are necessary to build a useful model.

name: Ubiquitous Language

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: ubiquitous-language Ubiquitous Language  A shared language refined by domain exp

summary: A shared language refined by domain experts and engineers so the model, conversations, tests, and code describe the same business reality.

name: Business Language

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: business-language Business Language  The everyday language of the business; it b

summary: The everyday language of the business; it becomes more reliable for software design when clarified into a bounded, testable model language.

name: Domain Model

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: domain-model Domain Model  A rich model for complex business behavior where enti

summary: A rich model for complex business behavior where entities, value objects, aggregates, events, and services preserve invariants and meaning.

name: Bounded Context

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: bounded-context Bounded Context  A boundary where a model and its language are i

summary: A boundary where a model and its language are internally consistent; outside the boundary, the same words may legitimately mean different things.

name: Context Map

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: context-map Context Map  A visual and conversational tool for showing bounded co

summary: A visual and conversational tool for showing bounded contexts, team ownership, upstream/downstream dependencies, and integration patterns.

name: Partnership

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: partnership Partnership  An integration relationship for teams that can coordina

summary: An integration relationship for teams that can coordinate closely and jointly evolve the interaction between their bounded contexts.

name: Shared Kernel

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: shared-kernel Shared Kernel  A deliberately small shared model slice between con

summary: A deliberately small shared model slice between contexts; useful only when teams can coordinate changes and keep the shared surface tiny.

name: Conformist

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: conformist Conformist  A downstream context accepts an upstream model when influ

summary: A downstream context accepts an upstream model when influence is low and the cost of insulating against it is not justified.

name: Anticorruption Layer

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: anticorruption-layer Anticorruption Layer  A translation boundary that protects

summary: A translation boundary that protects a local model from upstream concepts, data shapes, or language that would otherwise distort it.

name: Open Host Service

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: open-host-service Open Host Service  An upstream context publishes a stable inte

summary: An upstream context publishes a stable integration surface so multiple downstream consumers can integrate without bespoke coupling.

name: Transaction Script

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: transaction-script Transaction Script  A simple procedural style for straightfor

summary: A simple procedural style for straightforward business logic; it is usually appropriate before the domain behavior becomes deeply interconnected.

name: Active Record

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: active-record Active Record  A data-centric pattern that combines persistence-sh

summary: A data-centric pattern that combines persistence-shaped records with modest behavior; useful for simple CRUD-heavy domains but weak for complex invariants.

name: Entity

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: entity Entity  A model object identified by continuity and lifecycle rather than

summary: A model object identified by continuity and lifecycle rather than by all of its attributes.

name: Value Object

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: value-object Value Object  An immutable descriptive object identified by its val

summary: An immutable descriptive object identified by its value, useful for making business concepts explicit without identity overhead.

name: Aggregate

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: aggregate Aggregate  A consistency boundary around related domain objects where

summary: A consistency boundary around related domain objects where invariants are enforced through a root and transactions stay intentionally small.

name: Repository

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: repository Repository  A collection-like abstraction for loading and saving aggr

summary: A collection-like abstraction for loading and saving aggregates without leaking persistence mechanics into the domain model.

name: Domain Event

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: domain-event Domain Event  A record of a meaningful business fact that has happe

summary: A record of a meaningful business fact that has happened inside the domain and can trigger reactions without hiding the original model decision.

name: Event Sourcing

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: event-sourcing Event Sourcing  A modeling and persistence style where state is d

summary: A modeling and persistence style where state is derived from an append-only sequence of business events, useful when history itself is a first-class requirement.

name: Event Store

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: event-store Event Store  An append-only persistence mechanism for event-sourced

summary: An append-only persistence mechanism for event-sourced systems; it changes operational, query, migration, and audit tradeoffs.

name: CQRS

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: cqrs CQRS  A separation of command-side decisions from query-side read models, v

summary: A separation of command-side decisions from query-side read models, valuable when write consistency and read performance need different shapes.

name: Event Storming

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: event-storming Event Storming  A collaborative modeling workshop that discovers

summary: A collaborative modeling workshop that discovers domain events, commands, actors, policies, aggregates, and context boundaries through shared exploration.

name: Microservices

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: microservices Microservices  A deployment and ownership style that benefits from

summary: A deployment and ownership style that benefits from DDD boundaries but should not be derived mechanically from every model boundary.

name: Event-Driven Architecture

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: event-driven-architecture Event-Driven Architecture  An architecture style where

summary: An architecture style where systems integrate through events; DDD helps decide event meaning, ownership, and boundary protection.

name: Data Mesh

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: data-mesh Data Mesh  A domain-oriented data architecture that treats analytical

summary: A domain-oriented data architecture that treats analytical data ownership as a boundary and product concern rather than a central pipeline concern.

name: Subdomain refinement

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: practice-subdomain-refinement Subdomain refinement  하위 도메인은 큰 기능 목록을 나누는 데서 끝나지

summary: 하위 도메인은 큰 기능 목록을 나누는 데서 끝나지 않고, 복잡도와 전략적 의미가 보일 때까지 세분화하거나 멈추는 판단을 반복해야 한다.

name: Core subdomain investment decision

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: decision-core-subdomain-investment Core subdomain investment decision  핵심 하위 도메인

summary: 핵심 하위 도메인은 경쟁우위와 변동성이 높으므로 숙련된 인력과 고급 설계 노력을 집중하고, 일반/지원 하위 도메인과 같은 방식으로 외주화하거나 단순 처리하면 위험하다.

name: Ubiquitous language formulation

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: practice-ubiquitous-language-formulation Ubiquitous language formulation  유비쿼터스

summary: 유비쿼터스 언어는 기술 용어가 아니라 비즈니스 언어로 단일 의미를 유지해야 하며, 도메인 전문가와 계속 검증하면서 모델을 발전시키는 활동이다.

name: Domain discovery interview

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: practice-domain-discovery-interview Domain discovery interview  도메인 전문가와의 대화는 용어

summary: 도메인 전문가와의 대화는 용어, 규칙, 예외, 의사결정 흐름을 모델 후보로 끌어내는 탐색 활동이다.

name: Core domain pressure

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: concept-core-domain-pressure Core domain pressure  핵심 도메인은 경쟁력과 변경 압력이 높아 설계 투자와

summary: 핵심 도메인은 경쟁력과 변경 압력이 높아 설계 투자와 지식 축적 우선순위가 올라간다.

name: Generic solution fit

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: concept-generic-solution-fit Generic solution fit  일반 하위 도메인은 차별화보다 안정성과 비용 효율을

summary: 일반 하위 도메인은 차별화보다 안정성과 비용 효율을 중시해 외부 솔루션 적합성을 먼저 검토한다.

name: Language context split

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: boundary-language-context-split Language context split  같은 단어가 다른 의미를 갖는 지점은 bou

summary: 같은 단어가 다른 의미를 갖는 지점은 bounded context 분리를 검토해야 하는 강한 신호다.

name: Context map review

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: practice-context-map-review Context map review  context map은 팀과 모델 간 의존 관계를 드러내어

summary: context map은 팀과 모델 간 의존 관계를 드러내어 협력 방식과 통합 위험을 논의하게 한다.

name: Anticorruption translation

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: pattern-anticorruption-translation Anticorruption translation  외부 모델을 내부 언어로 번역하

summary: 외부 모델을 내부 언어로 번역하는 계층은 핵심 모델이 외부 의미에 오염되는 위험을 줄인다.

name: Aggregate consistency boundary

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: concept-aggregate-consistency-boundary Aggregate consistency boundary  aggregate

summary: aggregate는 모든 객체 묶음이 아니라 변경 불변식과 트랜잭션 일관성을 함께 지키는 경계다.

name: Domain event narrative

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: pattern-domain-event-narrative Domain event narrative  domain event는 이미 발생한 업무 사

summary: domain event는 이미 발생한 업무 사실을 모델 언어로 기록해 후속 반응과 통합을 느슨하게 연결한다.

name: Event storming slice

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: practice-event-storming-slice Event storming slice  event storming은 전체 시스템 설명보다

summary: event storming은 전체 시스템 설명보다 업무 흐름 slice를 중심으로 사건, 명령, 정책을 드러낸다.

name: CQRS read model fit

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: decision-cqrs-read-model-fit CQRS read model fit  CQRS는 조회와 명령의 변화 이유가 충분히 다를 때

summary: CQRS는 조회와 명령의 변화 이유가 충분히 다를 때 read model 최적화 선택지로 검토한다.

name: Anemic model drift

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: warning-anemic-model-drift Anemic model drift  도메인 규칙이 service 절차에만 머물면 모델이 데이터

summary: 도메인 규칙이 service 절차에만 머물면 모델이 데이터 구조로만 남고 지식 중심 설계 이점이 약해진다.

name: Team topology context

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: concept-team-topology-context Team topology context  bounded context는 코드 구조뿐 아니라

summary: bounded context는 코드 구조뿐 아니라 팀의 소통 경로와 책임 경계를 반영해야 유지된다.

name: Data product language

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: pattern-data-product-language Data product language  데이터 제품도 소유 팀, 소비자, 의미 계약을 명

summary: 데이터 제품도 소유 팀, 소비자, 의미 계약을 명시해야 도메인 언어와 분리되지 않는다.

name: Event-driven context fit

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: decision-event-driven-context-fit Event-driven context fit  event-driven 구조는 con

summary: event-driven 구조는 context 간 결합을 낮출 수 있지만 순서, 중복, 관측성 비용을 함께 평가해야 한다.

name: Model language review

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: practice-model-language-review Model language review  모델 이름과 코드 용어는 반복적으로 검토해 실제
